results.title: Cline Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
MakeHub.ai

used Cline to build MakeHub.ai

•3 reviews

Cline is simply the best AI coding agent out there. Part of my team's job is to test them all, and the Cline / Roo paradigm is great, and has been shaping the industry for the last 6 months, hence why Claude Code and other are copying them. The single alternative is Claude Code, but you can only use their. models, which are not currently SOTA, and it would be a mistake to bet that they will be SOTA all the time in the future.

results.title: Paraflow : The canvas-based product design agent. | Product Hunt
results.content:
🪄 Who is it for?

Indie builders — building fast with AI, but still care about logic and visual consistency.

Small teams — need to align PM, design, and engineering in real time.

Solo founders — juggle every role; must keep clarity and flow intact from 0→1.

🚀 See real cases

From sketch to a fitness app →

From a campaign brief to get a meetup landing page →

Expand a dashboard into five account states →

💪 We’re defining the next paradigm of product design 💪

Less prompts, more specs -- vibe coding in control.    
Paraflow is where this happens.

💡 We would love to hear your thoughts — not just on Paraflow, but on what AI teammates should be like in the products of tomorrow.

Basic practical tools are just as important as paradigm shifts. They feed each other :)

The AI video tools are getting crazier day by day! Even solving the brands' problems, too - ads and organic creatives. What impressed me most was how these weren't just "cool AI demos" but tools people can actually use right away. That seems to be the winning pattern now.

results.title: The transparent salary playbook | Product Hunt
results.content:
I had (and have) a level of privilege that meant I didn’t have to worry about friends, family, partners, or strangers on the internet knowing my take-home pay. Or, be concerned about how that level of pay transparency could impact future job offers.

It took me 9 years of scaling People Ops and pay transparency at early-stage startups to understand the nuance required for salary transparency and the paradigm shift from exposing numbers to putting in structure to reduce bias and pay inequity.

I’m going to narrow down the past 9 years of learning into one sentence: pay transparency should first and foremost be about giving your team a crystal clear understanding of why they are paid what they are paid and how they can grow in their role.
